semantic.role: Public facade that re-exports stable LLM runtime contracts and configuration resolution functions from lower-level infra modules, intended as the single import point for higher layers.
responsibilities: Re-export GlobalLLMConfig to provide access to the global LLM configuration singleton.
Re-export GlobalLLMSecrets to provide access to the global LLM secrets store.
Re-export getGlobalArchSpineDir to expose the ArchSpine global directory path.
Re-export assertResolvedLLMUsable to assert that a resolved LLM configuration is usable before making API calls.
Re-export createResolvedLLMClient to create an LLM client based on resolved settings.
Re-export providerRequiresApiKey to check whether a given provider requires an API key.
Re-export resolveLLMSettings to resolve user-provided LLM configuration against environment defaults.
Re-export LLMRuntimeOverrides, ResolvedLLMSettings, and ResolvedLLMValue type definitions to enable type-safe consumption of runtime settings.
outOfScope: Implementing actual LLM API calls or provider-specific logic.
Managing authentication tokens or secrets rotation.
Providing any UI for LLM configuration.
Performing validation or business logic beyond re-exporting resolved contracts.
invariants: Higher layers must import from this facade instead of reaching into src/infra/llm/ directly, ensuring stable import surface and safe infra refactors.
The re-exported symbols must remain stable and backward-compatible to avoid breaking downstream consumers.

changeIntent.architecturalIntent: Establish a stable, single-entry-point facade for LLM runtime contracts and configuration resolution, decoupling higher layers from internal implementation details and aligning with the architectural governance principles of ArchSpine.
changeIntent.recentChangeIntent: Part of the v1.0.0 release that introduced an architectural governance and semantic layer for the AI era, establishing clean public facades for infrastructure modules.

publicSurface.description: Exposes the singleton global LLM configuration object, allowing read access to default provider, model, and endpoint settings.
Exposes the global LLM secrets store, providing read access to API keys and sensitive credentials for configured providers.
Returns the absolute path to the global ArchSpine directory (e.g., ~/.archspine) used for storing config and runtime data.
Validates that a resolved LLM settings object has all required fields and throws if the configuration is not usable for making API calls.
Factory function that creates an LLM client instance (e.g., OpenAI, Gemini) based on the provided resolved settings and optional overrides.
Checks whether a given LLM provider name (e.g., 'openai', 'gemini') requires an API key to be set before making requests.
Merges user-provided LLM configuration overrides with global/default settings and environment variables, returning a fully resolved settings object.
Type definition for optional overrides (provider, model, etc.) that can be passed to createResolvedLLMClient at runtime.
Type definition representing the fully resolved LLM configuration object with all required fields populated.
Type definition for a single resolved LLM instance configuration, typically embedded within a multi-model setup.
